meaning: adj.爱打架的,斗志旺盛的,细小的,碎屑的,杂凑的,不连贯的
examples: The note was written on a scrappy bit of paper.
The final chapter is no more than a scrappy addition.
Ruthie's hand rests damp in Kate's, and together they watch two scrappy fairies race by, the swifter one waving a long string of raffle tickets.
She's the woman who built eBay from a scrappy start-up to a global force.
Fordham, by contrast, was a team of scrappy kids from the Bronx and Brooklyn.
